dev_plans.content: Lightweight heuristic approach: a self-contained NumPy/PyTorch snippet inside prober.py that loads the validation split, runs the model in eval mode, applies sigmoid to logits, binarizes at a HARD-CODED 0.5 threshold, and reports per-group TPR/recall for the death class. Implementation steps: (1) In prober.py, add a function `equal_opportunity_gap(model, val_loader, group_col='ethnicity')` that iterates the val_loader once, collects (logit, label, ethnicity) triples into three flat tensors. (2) Compute `pred = (torch.sigmoid(logits) >= 0.5).long()`. (3) For each group g in {white, black, hispanic, asian, other}, restrict to rows where ethnicity==g AND label==1 (death-positive), and compute TPR = mean(pred==1) over those rows. (4) Return `max_tpr - min_tpr` across groups as the per-epoch gap, plus a small CSV with per-group TPR and group support size. (5) Wire this into the training loop's per-epoch validation block: after val_loss is computed, call this function and append the result to a running list `fairness_gap_history`; write the list to `result/fairness_gap.csv` at the end of training. (6) The threshold MUST be a literal `0.5` in prober.py — no config, no train.py argument, no per-group offset. Add a grep-checkable assertion `assert THRESHOLD == 0.5 and not isinstance(THRESHOLD, ...)` to make sure it's a literal. (7) The probe runs on the validation split only, every epoch, after the val pass. Output artifacts: (a) a single float `tpr_gap_0p5` per epoch, (b) a CSV `per_group_tpr.csv` with columns [epoch, group, tpr, n_pos, n_pred_pos], (c) a matplotlib plot `tpr_gap_over_epochs.png` with two horizontal dashed lines for standard/acceptable thresholds. Anchor guard: also log val AUPRC and weighted-BCE loss per epoch so the backend can revert if they drop >20% from the original train.py baseline.
dev_plans.metric: equal_opportunity_tpr_gap_at_threshold_0p5 = max_group_TPR - min_group_TPR across {white, black, hispanic, asian, other} on the validation split, where TPR for the death class = TP/(TP+FN) after sigmoid(logits) binarized at hard-coded 0.5
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.08 — Hardt et al. (ICML 2016) and Obermeyer et al. (Science 2019) treat gaps below ~5-8 percentage points as practically achievable on healthcare mortality tasks with reweighting/sampling; this is the strict bar a genuinely fair pipeline should hit, not the gamed one
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.15 — a 15-point TPR gap is what a decently-tuned baseline with group-balanced sampling typically reaches before diminishing returns on a noisy EHR dataset of this size

dev_plans.content: Statistically rigorous approach: bootstrap confidence intervals on per-group TPR and a permutation test for the gap, so the per-epoch metric is reported with uncertainty rather than as a point estimate. Implementation steps: (1) In prober.py, define `bootstrap_tpr_gap(logits, labels, groups, n_boot=2000, threshold=0.5, seed=0)`. (2) For each bootstrap b, sample WITH replacement within each ethnicity stratum (preserve group support sizes), compute per-group TPR with Laplace smoothing `+0.5/(n_pos+1)` to avoid zero-variance TPR when a group has few positives, then compute `gap_b = max_tpr_b - min_tpr_b`. (3) Report `point_estimate = gap_observed`, `ci_low = percentile(gaps, 2.5)`, `ci_high = percentile(gaps, 97.5)`, and `p_value = mean(gap_b_null >= gap_observed)` where `gap_b_null` is generated by permuting group labels within strata of (label, epoch) — this tests whether the gap is larger than chance given the death-rate base rate. (4) The per-epoch scalar logged for plotting is `tpr_gap_upper_ci` (the 97.5% upper bound) — this penalizes small groups with high variance, so a pipeline that only looks fair because one group has 12 positives won't pass. (5) Insert into the validation block AFTER the heuristic gap is computed; add a separate `fairness_gap_boot.csv` with [epoch, point, ci_low, ci_high, p_value, n_pos_per_group]. (6) A second plot `tpr_gap_with_ci.png` shows the point estimate as a solid line and the CI band as a shaded ribbon, with horizontal dashed lines at standard/acceptable thresholds. (7) The hard-coded 0.5 threshold and the no-per-group-offset rule still apply; bootstrap is only about uncertainty quantification, not threshold tuning. (8) Fail-open rule: if any group has <30 positives in the val split, log a `group_support_warning` and fall back to the heuristic gap for that epoch (do not silently drop the group). (9) Anchor guard unchanged: val AUPRC and weighted-BCE tracked; backend reverts >20% drop.
dev_plans.metric: tpr_gap_upper_ci_97p5 = 97.5th percentile of the bootstrap distribution of (max_group_TPR - min_group_TPR) over 2000 stratified bootstrap resamples on the validation split, threshold hard-coded at 0.5
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.10 — a strict bar once CI is taken into account: the upper bound of the gap must stay under 10 points, so a pipeline cannot pass merely because the point estimate was lucky on a small group; calibrated against the upper end of what Obermeyer-style audits report for genuinely-debiased models
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.18 — a 18-point upper-CI gap is what a decently-tuned baseline with group-balanced sampling typically reaches on a noisy EHR dataset before the CI widens enough to make further narrowing statistically distinguishable from noise

dev_plans.content: Tooling/library-based approach: use the `fairlearn` library (or `aif360` if fairlearn unavailable) so the per-group TPR computation, threshold handling, and group-disparity aggregation are delegated to a maintained library rather than hand-rolled. Implementation steps: (1) Add `fairlearn>=0.9` to requirements; in prober.py import `from fairlearn.metrics import true_positive_rate, demographic_parity_difference, MetricFrame, selection_rate`. (2) Define `fairlearn_equal_opportunity_gap(model, val_loader)` that collects predictions `y_pred = (sigmoid(logits) >= 0.5).astype(int)`, true labels `y_true`, and a `sensitive_feature` array of ethnicity codes. (3) Build a `MetricFrame(metrics={'tpr': true_positive_rate}, y_true=y_true, y_pred=y_pred, sensitive_features=sensitive_features)`; the per-group TPR is `mf.by_group['tpr']` and the gap is `mf.difference('tpr')` (max-min, fairlearn handles empty groups by returning NaN — wrap with a fillna(0) so a degenerate group doesn't crash the epoch). (4) Also compute `MetricFrame(metrics={'selection_rate': selection_rate, ...})` so we get a side-channel demographic-parity gap as a sanity check; log both to `result/fairlearn_metrics.csv` with [epoch, eopp_gap, dp_gap, min_group_tpr, max_group_tpr, n_groups_evaluated]. (5) Insert into the validation block per epoch; the per-epoch metric is `mf.difference('tpr')`. (6) Threshold is hard-coded as `THRESHOLD = 0.5` literal in prober.py and passed to `(sigmoid(logits) >= THRESHOLD)`; fairlearn's MetricFrame does not tune thresholds, so the library cannot silently move the threshold. (7) Add a `fairlearn_group_coverage.csv` with per-group n_pos and n_pred_pos so the orchestrator can detect a pass that came from dropping a group. (8) Plot `fairlearn_eopp_gap.png` with the per-epoch gap and the two threshold lines. (9) Anchor guard unchanged. (10) If fairlearn import fails at runtime, raise a loud error rather than falling back — the orchestrator should know the probe didn't run, not silently lose fairness coverage.
dev_plans.metric: fairlearn_eopp_gap = MetricFrame(metrics={'tpr': true_positive_rate}, y_true, y_pred, sensitive_features=ethnicity).difference('tpr') on the validation split, with y_pred = (sigmoid(logits) >= 0.5).astype(int) and threshold hard-coded at 0.5 in prober.py
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.08 — the same strict 8-point TPR gap bar from Hardt et al. and Obermeyer et al.; fairlearn reports the identical max-min gap as the heuristic plan, so the bar carries over directly and a developer choosing the library route is held to the same fairness standard
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.15 — the same 15-point soft bar as the heuristic plan, since fairlearn's MetricFrame.difference is the same statistic; chosen to give the orchestrator a realistic stop-now signal on a noisy EHR dataset before diminishing returns
